prompt:
Change request from the booking desk. Our crewing-agency berth board (CrewBoard/Posting in this directory, plain JDK, sibling files, source launcher, no build tool) does post + search fine, and the mariners' phone app is built on search() exactly as it behaves today — that behavior is frozen. But the desk keeps hearing the same three complaints: good berths are buried (no relevance), mariners can't see 'how many of these are tugs / what ranks are hiring' at a glance (no facets), and the app pages by re-running the query with an offset, so when a captain posts mid-scroll the list shifts and people see duplicates. I want ranked search with facet counts and cursor pagination, server-side, deterministic.

Add a method to CrewBoard: public SearchPage find(SearchRequest req). New sibling files:

SearchRequest.java — public record SearchRequest(String query, int pageSize, String cursor). pageSize < 1 -> IllegalArgumentException with message exactly "pageSize must be positive". cursor null means first page.

Hit.java — public record Hit(Posting posting, int score).

SearchPage.java — hits() returns List<Hit> (the page, in rank order), facets() returns Map<String, Map<String, Integer>>, nextCursor() returns the cursor for the next page or null when this page ends the list.

Semantics, all pinned by TestMain:

MATCHING — find() matches exactly the postings search() matches (same term split, same case-insensitive title-or-description substring AND). Lowercase with Locale.ROOT like the rest of the file. Blank/null query matches the whole board.

SCORING — per DISTINCT query term (duplicate terms in the query count once): +3 if the term occurs in the title, +2 if it equals the vesselType (case-insensitive), +1 if it occurs in the description. A posting's score is the sum over terms; a blank query scores everything 0. Rank order: score desc, then postedDay desc, then id asc.

FACETS — computed over the FULL match set (not just the returned page), three dimensions in exactly this outer order: vesselType, rank, homePort. Each dimension maps facet value -> matching-posting count, entries ordered by count desc then value asc. Use insertion-ordered maps so toString() shows that order. An empty match set still reports all three dimensions (empty).

CURSOR — nextCursor() must be an OPAQUE token (no raw posting ids or scores readable in it) encoding the rank-order position of the last hit on the page. find() with a cursor returns hits strictly AFTER that position in the (score desc, postedDay desc, id asc) order, evaluated against the board's CURRENT contents: berths posted after the cursor was handed out appear on later pages if they rank after the cursor position, and do NOT shuffle or duplicate anything if they rank before it. Walking pages until nextCursor() is null visits every hit exactly once. An unparseable or wrong-shaped cursor -> IllegalArgumentException with message exactly "bad cursor".

Don't touch Posting.java or the existing post/size/search code paths' behavior — RegressionTests.java pins them (run just that suite with `java RegressionTests.java`). TestMain.java runs regression + feature and is the acceptance contract. Both test files are off limits. Done = `java TestMain.java` all PASS, exit 0.
